emergent_themes.description: The deliberate preservation of Friday takeaway as a special weekly ritual, with resistance to expanding to other days to maintain its emotional significance
Extreme sensitivity to small price differences (50p) driving platform switching behavior, reflecting tight budget constraints
Cooking viewed as an enjoyable hobby and creative experimentation rather than just necessity, providing emotional satisfaction
Family members providing financial support for takeaway orders, creating social connection and permission to indulge
Fear of committing to subscriptions due to uncertainty about future usage patterns and potential financial loss

coding_summary: Josh presents as an extremely budget-conscious first-year medicine student who has developed sophisticated strategies around food and delivery. His £15-20 weekly food budget drives micro-level price sensitivity (comparing apps for 50p differences) while maintaining a sacred Friday takeaway ritual that serves as emotional reward and family connection. He views cooking as a creative hobby rather than chore, using social media inspiration and reduced-price ingredients. His delivery behavior is highly methodical - preferring Just Eat for independent restaurants over chains, always price-comparing, and avoiding subscriptions due to usage uncertainty. Family influence is significant, both in providing takeaway funding and preventing him from becoming a delivery rider due to safety concerns. Post-graduation, he expects increased spending but remains uncertain about time availability.
